title: Catalytic Enhancement of Coherence Fraction in Noisy Quantum Channels and Characterization of Strictly Incoherent Operations
authors: Priyabrata Char, Dipayan Chakraborty, Indrani Chattopadhyay, Debasis Sarkar
abstract: In realistic quantum information processing tasks, quantum states are inevitably affected by environmental noise, leading to decoherence and degradation of useful quantum resources. The coherence fraction, which serves as an important figure of merit for several quantum protocols, may decrease significantly after the action of a noisy channel. Such degradation can result in unsatisfactory performance in real-world applications. In this work, we investigate whether catalysis can be used to pre-process the input state to enhance the coherence fraction of an output state from a quantum channel. Specifically, we study whether using a processed state $ρ_s'$ as the input to a quantum channel $Λ$, instead of the original state $ρ_s$, can yield an output state $Λ(ρ_s')$ whose coherence fraction exceeds that of $Λ(ρ_s)$. We analyze the conditions under which such an improvement is possible. We also provide a practical application of our setup for the phase discrimination task. Furthermore, we establish a necessary and sufficient condition for an incoherent state preserving CPTP(Completely Positive Trace Preserving) map $\mathcal{E}$ to be a particular type of Strictly Incoherent Operation (SIO). This characterization provides a new structural understanding of SIO and clarifies its role in coherence manipulation. Our results offer practical insights into coherence preservation and enhancement in noisy quantum processes and may be useful for optimizing quantum information protocols under realistic conditions. We also provide numerical examples to support our claims.
summary: This paper explores how quantum catalysis can be used to protect or even enhance the coherence fraction of a quantum state when it passes through a noisy channel. By pre-processing the input state using an auxiliary catalyst, the authors show that the output coherence can be higher than if the original state were used. Additionally, the work provides a new structural characterization of a specific class of incoherent operations. These findings offer practical insights for optimizing quantum protocols in realistic, noisy environments.
main_problem: Investigating whether quantum catalysis can be used to pre-process input states to enhance the coherence fraction of an output state after passing through a noisy quantum channel, and characterizing the structural properties of Strictly Incoherent Operations (SIO).
main_result: The authors prove that correlated catalysis can prevent the degradation of the coherence fraction in noisy channels and establish a necessary and sufficient condition for a CPTP map to be a Schur-multiplier type SIO.
method: The study uses the framework of Quantum Resource Theory (QRT) of coherence, employing analytical proofs, algebraic derivations (such as polynomial interpolation), and numerical simulations using the Ginibre ensemble.
model_or_system: The work focuses on single-party d-dimensional quantum systems subject to noisy CPTP channels, specifically including the quantum addition channel and the qutrit phase-damping channel.
key_observables: Coherence fraction (CF), l1 norm of coherence, robustness of coherence, and success probability in phase discrimination tasks.
important_parameters: Noise parameter (p) for dephasing channels, the parameter (alpha) for the quantum addition channel, and the regularized coherence fraction.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the use of the coherence fraction as the primary figure of merit and focuses on the regime of noisy channels that reduce coherence.
figures_summary: Figures 1-3 compare the coherence fraction of standard versus processed states under different noise levels; Figure 4 is a scatter plot showing the Frobenius norm deviation to demonstrate that the multiplicative property fails for SIOs that are not Schur multipliers.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of coherence degradation, defines the catalytic scheme and operational classes (MIO, IO, SIO), provides analytical theorems for coherence enhancement, applies the results to phase discrimination, characterizes the SIO class via a multiplicative condition, and concludes with numerical validation.
why_interesting: This paper is highly relevant to open quantum systems and quantum optics as it provides a theoretical method (catalysis) to mitigate the effects of environmental noise on a key quantum resource (coherence).
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 4: Frobenius-norm deviation ∥E(ρk)E(σ)−E(ρkσ)∥F for randomly generated density matrices ρk. The incoherent state σ = diag(0.3, 0.7) is non-degenerate. The SIO E is not of Schur-multiplier form. The nonzero deviations confirm viola- tion of the multiplicative relation.
